rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Foster Wheeler To For A Protective Order Regarding The Deposition Of Plaintiff; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 31, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Foster Wheeler LLC¿s motion for a protective order regarding the deposition of plaintiff is denied. Defendant¿s amended motion stated that it superseded this prior-filed motion, making it moot. Even if the court considers this motion as timely and on its merits, it is denied based on defendant¿s failure to demonstrate that it properly met and conferred prior to filing the motion as required by C.C.P. 2025.420(a). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:15am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Foster Wheeler To For A Protective Order Regarding The Deposition Of Plaintiff; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 31, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Foster Wheeler LLC¿s amended motion for a protective order regarding the deposition of plaintiff is denied. Defendant failed to provide proper notice of its amended motion because it failed to add 2 additional days for electronic service pursuant to General Order 158, C.C.P. 1010.6 and CRC 2.251(f). Even if the portion of the motion seeking a protective order is considered as timely based on defendant¿s initial notice of January 3, it is denied based on defendant¿s failure to demonstrate that it properly met and conferred prior to filing the motion as required by C.C.P. 2025.420(a). The portion of the amended motion which added a motion to stay or dismiss on the grounds of forum non conveniens that was not included in the initial motion is denied as untimely. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:15am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant Foster Wheelers Amended Motion For Protective Order Regarding Deposition Of Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 31, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant E.F. Brady Company, Inc.¿s joinder in Foster Wheeler LLC¿s amended motion for a protective order regarding the deposition of plaintiff is off calendar as moot given the ruling on the underlying motion. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CARROLL WILLIAM PEIL et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Production Of Nibbi Bros. Inc.S Custodian(S) Of Records And Person(S) Most Qualified For Deposition And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 31, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel production of Nibbi Bros. Inc.¿s custodian of records and person most knowledgeable for deposition and request for production of documents is granted. Defendant did not substantively oppose the motion. Defendant¿s condition requiring further meet and confer efforts is denied. The deposition and document production must occur 30 days from the date of this hearing. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions of $1066 is granted. Defendant did not oppose the imposition of sanctions. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:15am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
